opportunity.title: Jeriko – an AI agent that runs directly inside your OS
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The real challenge isn't the AI layer itself but achieving sub-second latency for native OS calls while maintaining sandbox isolation. Most "native" agents I've seen either offload to HTTP or use unstable C-APIs that break across OS updates. Consider a hybrid approach: compile plugins as native binaries for core OS features, then use a capability graph to route tool calls at runtime—this lets you update agent logic without recompiling native components.
actions.reasoning: I have built multiple AI agent systems with native OS integration, tool orchestration, and dynamic capability discovery—giving me practical experience with the architecture tradeoffs discussed here.
